title: Viewport selection + hover + interaction
analysis:
Task 95 adds interactive 3D selection to the viewport. The existing codebase follows a clean factory-function pattern: scene.ts, controls.ts, meshManager.ts each export a `create*` function returning a context object with methods and a dispose. Tests mock three.js via vi.mock('three') with inline mock classes.

The core new module is `selection.ts` — a `createSelection` factory that wraps Three.js Raycaster for pointer-based hover/click detection on meshes managed by meshManager. It will: (1) resolve pointer coords to entity paths via raycasting, (2) apply emissive highlight on hover, (3) create WireframeGeometry+LineSegments overlay on selection, (4) provide fitToView to frame all content, and (5) expose dispose for cleanup. Viewport.tsx will be extended to wire selection into the render loop, render a floating tooltip on hover, an evaluating spinner overlay when evalStatus.phase !== idle, and a fit-to-view button.

Key considerations: The meshManager already stores entity_path as mesh.name and exposes getSceneMeshes() returning Map<string, Mesh> — perfect for raycasting. The selectionStore has selectEntity/hoverEntity. The engineStore has evalStatus with phase 'idle'|'evaluating'|'resolving'. All Three.js dependencies will be mocked in tests following the established pattern.

The Viewport component currently accepts only {meshes} prop. It will be extended to accept optional callbacks (onHover, onSelect), evalStatus, hoveredEntity, selectedEntity, and a fitToView ref. This keeps the component testable with simple prop injection, matching the existing pattern from task 93 where stores are created in App and passed as props.

--- REVIEW FIX ADDENDUM (round 1) ---
Post-implementation review identified two blocking issues:
1. Fit-to-view button broken: onClick calls props.onFitToView?.() but never selection.fitToView(). Fix: mutable ref pattern.
2. Material resource leak: removeWireframe() disposes WireframeGeometry but not LineBasicMaterial. Fix: add material.dispose().

--- REVIEW FIX ADDENDUM (round 2) ---
Second review identified three additional blocking performance/robustness issues:
3. hot_path_layout_thrashing: getBoundingClientRect() called on every pointermove forces synchronous layout reflow at mouse sampling rate (60-1000 Hz). Fix: cache DOMRect in closure, invalidate lazily on resize via a ResizeObserver or an onResize() method that Viewport.tsx calls from its existing ResizeObserver.
4. hot_path_missing_raf_throttle: Full raycast (setFromCamera + intersectObjects) runs synchronously on every pointermove with no frame-rate gating. At 1000 Hz with even modest scenes, this saturates the main thread. Fix: store pendingMoveEvent in closure, schedule rAF only if not already queued, consume latest event in rAF callback. Cancel outstanding rAF in dispose().
5. stale_resource_state: Wireframe overlay uses WireframeGeometry snapshot from construction time. When meshManager.sync() updates geometry after engine re-evaluation, the wireframe shows the old shape. The selectedEntity effect in Viewport.tsx doesn't track mesh data changes. Fix: make the selectedEntity effect also depend on props.meshes so it re-runs setSelected() when geometry changes, rebuilding the wireframe.

steps.description: Write failing tests for createSelection factory skeleton: returns object with setHovered, setSelected, fitToView, dispose methods. Mock Three.js Raycaster, WireframeGeometry, LineSegments, LineBasicMaterial, Box3, Vector3, Color. Follow same vi.mock('three') pattern as meshManager.test.ts.

Implement createSelection factory skeleton in selection.ts. Accepts {scene, camera, domElement, getMeshes: () => Map<string, Mesh>, onHover: (path: string|null) => void, onSelect: (path: string|null) => void}. Returns SelectionContext with stub methods (setHovered, setSelected, fitToView, dispose). Export from viewport/index.ts.

Write failing tests for pointer-based hover raycasting: simulate pointermove event on domElement, verify Raycaster.setFromCamera called with normalized device coordinates computed from event clientX/Y relative to domElement bounds, and raycaster.intersectObjects called with mesh array from getMeshes(). When intersection found, onHover called with mesh.name. When no intersection, onHover called with null.

Implement pointermove handler in createSelection: compute NDC from event coords and domElement rect, call raycaster.setFromCamera + intersectObjects on Array.from(getMeshes().values()), call onHover with first intersection's object.name or null. Register handler via domElement.addEventListener('pointermove', ...).

Write failing tests for hover emissive highlight: setHovered('A') applies emissive color (theme accent) to mesh A's MeshStandardMaterial. setHovered(null) resets emissive to black (0x000000). Changing hover from A to B resets A's emissive and highlights B's emissive. setHovered with unknown entity path is a no-op.

Implement setHovered in createSelection: track previousHoveredPath, look up mesh in getMeshes(), reset previous mesh's material.emissive to Color(0x000000), apply highlight Color to new mesh's material.emissive. Store highlight color as constant derived from THEME_TOKENS.accent.

Write failing tests for click-based selection raycasting: simulate pointerdown event on domElement, verify raycaster used same as hover, onSelect called with mesh.name on intersection, onSelect(null) on miss.
Implement pointerdown handler in createSelection: same raycast logic as hover, call onSelect. Register via domElement.addEventListener('pointerdown', ...).

Write failing tests for wireframe overlay on selection: setSelected('A') creates WireframeGeometry from mesh A's geometry, creates LineSegments with LineBasicMaterial(accent color), adds LineSegments to scene. setSelected(null) removes LineSegments from scene and disposes WireframeGeometry. Changing selection from A to B removes A's wireframe, creates B's. setSelected with unknown entity is a no-op (no wireframe, no crash).

Implement setSelected in createSelection: track current wireframe LineSegments object. On deselect, remove from scene and dispose geometry. On select, create WireframeGeometry from mesh.geometry, create LineSegments with LineBasicMaterial({color: accent}), add to scene.

Write failing tests for fitToView: with meshes in scene, fitToView computes bounding box via Box3.expandByObject for each mesh, calculates center and size, positions camera at appropriate distance based on bounding sphere radius and camera FOV, calls camera.lookAt(center). With no meshes, fitToView is a no-op (no crash).

Implement fitToView in createSelection: create Box3, iterate getMeshes() calling box.expandByObject(mesh), compute center via box.getCenter, compute size via box.getSize, calculate distance = max(sizeX, sizeY, sizeZ) / (2 * tan(camera.fov/2 * PI/180)), set camera.position to center offset by distance on Z, call camera.lookAt(center), camera.updateProjectionMatrix().
Write failing tests for dispose: verify domElement.removeEventListener called for both 'pointermove' and 'pointerdown' handlers. If wireframe exists, it is removed from scene and disposed.
Implement dispose in createSelection: remove pointermove and pointerdown listeners from domElement, clean up wireframe if present (remove from scene, dispose geometry).

Write failing tests for updated Viewport.tsx: (1) Viewport with onHover/onSelect props wires them through createSelection, (2) tooltip div with data-testid='viewport-tooltip' appears when hoveredEntity prop is set and shows the entity name, (3) tooltip hidden when hoveredEntity is null, (4) spinner overlay with data-testid='viewport-spinner' appears when evalStatus.phase is 'evaluating', (5) spinner hidden when evalStatus.phase is 'idle', (6) fit-to-view button with data-testid='fit-to-view' exists.

Update Viewport.tsx: extend ViewportProps with optional onHover, onSelect, hoveredEntity, selectedEntity, evalStatus, onFitToView. In onMount, create selection via createSelection, wire onHover/onSelect. Add createEffect watching hoveredEntity → call selection.setHovered, createEffect watching selectedEntity → call selection.setSelected. Render tooltip overlay (Show when hoveredEntity), spinner overlay (Show when evalStatus?.phase !== 'idle'), fit-to-view button. Dispose selection in onCleanup. Update viewport/index.ts exports for SelectionContext.

REVIEW FIX: Write failing tests for two issues: (1) In selection.test.ts, add test 'removeWireframe disposes LineBasicMaterial' — call setSelected('A') then setSelected(null), assert wireframe.material.dispose was called. Add test 'dispose disposes wireframe material if wireframe exists' — call setSelected('A') then dispose(), assert wireframe.material.dispose was called. Add test 'changing selection disposes previous wireframe material' — call setSelected('A') then setSelected('B'), assert first wireframe's material.dispose was called. (2) In Viewport.test.tsx, add test 'clicking fit-to-view button calls selection.fitToView' — render Viewport, find button by data-testid 'fit-to-view', fireEvent.click it, assert mockSelectionFitToView was called. Also assert props.onFitToView callback is still called (both should fire).

REVIEW FIX: Fix both issues: (1) In selection.ts removeWireframe(), add `(currentWireframe.material as LineBasicMaterial).dispose()` after geometry.dispose() and before nulling the reference. This ensures the material is freed on every wireframe removal (deselect, re-select, and dispose). (2) In Viewport.tsx, declare `let doFitToView: (() => void) | undefined` in the Viewport function scope before onMount. Inside onMount, after creating `selection`, assign `doFitToView = () => selection.fitToView()`. Change the button onClick from `() => props.onFitToView?.()` to `() => { doFitToView?.(); props.onFitToView?.(); }`. This bridges the onMount-scoped selection instance to the JSX click handler via a mutable ref while keeping selection private to the mount closure.

REVIEW FIX (hot_path_layout_thrashing): Write failing tests in selection.test.ts for cached DOMRect behavior. (1) 'computeNDC uses cached rect and does not call getBoundingClientRect on every pointermove' — dispatch two pointermove events, assert domElement.getBoundingClientRect was called only once (lazy population on first event). (2) 'invalidateRect causes next pointermove to re-read getBoundingClientRect' — dispatch pointermove (populates cache), call selection.invalidateRect(), dispatch another pointermove, assert getBoundingClientRect was called twice total. (3) 'pointerdown also uses cached rect' — dispatch pointerdown after pointermove, assert getBoundingClientRect still called only once. Add invalidateRect to the SelectionContext interface assertion in the factory skeleton test. Spy on domElement.getBoundingClientRect to count calls.

REVIEW FIX (hot_path_layout_thrashing): In selection.ts, add `let cachedRect: DOMRect | null = null` in the factory closure. Replace `domElement.getBoundingClientRect()` in computeNDC with a helper `getRect()` that returns cachedRect if non-null, otherwise calls `cachedRect = domElement.getBoundingClientRect()` and returns it. Add `invalidateRect()` method that sets `cachedRect = null`. Export invalidateRect on SelectionContext interface. In Viewport.tsx, call `selection.invalidateRect()` inside the existing ResizeObserver callback (alongside the existing `resize(w, h)` call), so the rect cache is invalidated whenever the viewport container resizes.

REVIEW FIX (hot_path_missing_raf_throttle): Write failing tests in selection.test.ts for rAF-throttled pointermove raycasting. (1) 'pointermove does not raycast synchronously — stores pending event and schedules rAF' — dispatch pointermove, assert raycaster.setFromCamera was NOT called synchronously, assert requestAnimationFrame was called. (2) 'rAF callback performs raycast with latest pending event' — dispatch pointermove, invoke the captured rAF callback, assert raycaster.setFromCamera was called once and onHover was called. (3) 'multiple rapid pointermoves only schedule one rAF and raycast uses the last event' — dispatch 3 pointermove events with different clientX, assert requestAnimationFrame was called only once, invoke rAF callback, assert raycaster.setFromCamera called once with NDC from the last event's coords. (4) 'pointerdown still raycasts synchronously (not throttled)' — dispatch pointerdown, assert raycaster.setFromCamera called immediately without rAF. (5) 'dispose cancels outstanding rAF' — dispatch pointermove, call dispose(), assert cancelAnimationFrame was called with the rAF id. Mock requestAnimationFrame and cancelAnimationFrame in the test setup.

REVIEW FIX (hot_path_missing_raf_throttle): In selection.ts, refactor handlePointerMove to use pending-event + rAF throttle pattern. Add closure variables: `let pendingMoveEvent: Event | null = null; let hoverRafId = 0;`. In handlePointerMove, assign `pendingMoveEvent = event`, then only if `hoverRafId === 0`, schedule `hoverRafId = requestAnimationFrame(processHover)`. The `processHover` function reads `pendingMoveEvent`, clears it and `hoverRafId`, then performs the raycast + onHover call. pointerdown handler remains synchronous (click selection should not be delayed). In dispose(), add `if (hoverRafId) cancelAnimationFrame(hoverRafId)` before removing listeners.

REVIEW FIX (stale_resource_state): Write failing test in Viewport.test.tsx: 'selectedEntity effect re-runs setSelected when props.meshes changes' — render Viewport with selectedEntity='A' and meshes={initial}, assert mockSelectionSetSelected was called with 'A'. Then update props.meshes to a new object (simulating engine re-evaluation), assert mockSelectionSetSelected was called again with 'A' (rebuilding the wireframe from updated geometry). Use SolidJS createSignal to drive reactive prop changes in the test.

REVIEW FIX (stale_resource_state): In Viewport.tsx, modify the createEffect that watches selectedEntity to also read props.meshes, so it re-runs when either changes. Change from `createEffect(() => { selection.setSelected(props.selectedEntity ?? null); });` to `createEffect(() => { void props.meshes; selection.setSelected(props.selectedEntity ?? null); });`. This ensures that when meshManager.sync() processes updated geometry (triggered by the meshes effect), the selection effect also re-fires, causing setSelected to rebuild the WireframeGeometry from the now-updated mesh geometry. Also update the Viewport.test.tsx mock for selection module to include invalidateRect (from step-20) so existing tests don't break.

design_decisions.decision: Create selection.ts as a separate factory module following the same create* pattern as scene.ts, controls.ts, meshManager.ts
design_decisions.rationale: Consistent with the established viewport module architecture. Each concern is isolated in its own factory, making testing straightforward with vi.mock.

design_decisions.decision: Selection module takes callback functions (onHover, onSelect) rather than directly importing selectionStore
design_decisions.rationale: Keeps the selection module decoupled from SolidJS stores. The Viewport component wires callbacks to store methods, making the selection module purely Three.js-focused and easier to test without SolidJS reactivity.

design_decisions.decision: Separate setHovered/setSelected from pointer event handlers
design_decisions.rationale: Allows the store to drive visual state (highlight/wireframe) independently of pointer events. When selection changes programmatically (e.g., from property editor click), the wireframe and highlight can still be updated via props flowing through Viewport. Also enables easier testing of visual effects vs event handling.

design_decisions.decision: Use pointer events (pointermove, pointerdown) instead of mouse events
design_decisions.rationale: Pointer events are the modern standard, work with touch and stylus, and are used by OrbitControls internally. Consistent event model avoids conflicts.

design_decisions.decision: Wireframe uses LineBasicMaterial with theme accent color
design_decisions.rationale: Matches the Catppuccin Mocha theme already in use (THEME_TOKENS.accent = '#89b4fa'). WireframeGeometry+LineSegments is the standard Three.js approach for wireframe overlays that don't affect the original mesh material.

design_decisions.decision: fitToView is synchronous camera repositioning (not animated) in the initial implementation
design_decisions.rationale: Animation (tweening) requires either a dependency like tween.js or manual interpolation in the render loop. Starting with instant repositioning keeps complexity low; animation can be added later as an enhancement.

design_decisions.decision: Tooltip and spinner are absolutely-positioned divs inside the viewport container
design_decisions.rationale: CSS absolute positioning over the canvas is the standard pattern for 3D viewport overlays. No extra dependency needed. Styled with existing theme CSS variables.

design_decisions.decision: evalStatus, hoveredEntity, selectedEntity passed as props to Viewport
design_decisions.rationale: Keeps the component testable with simple prop injection. The parent (App.tsx, from task 93) already has both stores and can thread them through. Avoids premature context coupling. Matches existing pattern where meshes are a prop.

design_decisions.decision: Use mutable ref (doFitToView) to bridge onMount-scoped selection to JSX button handler
design_decisions.rationale: The selection instance is created inside onMount and cannot be directly referenced in JSX (which is returned synchronously before onMount runs). A mutable variable declared in the component function scope, assigned inside onMount, is the standard SolidJS pattern for this. It keeps the selection instance private to the mount closure while exposing only the fitToView action through a stable function pointer.

design_decisions.decision: Dispose both geometry AND material in removeWireframe()
design_decisions.rationale: Three.js materials are GPU-resident resources that must be explicitly disposed. The original code only disposed geometry, leaking LineBasicMaterial on every selection change. This matches the dispose pattern already used in meshManager.ts.

design_decisions.decision: Cache DOMRect in closure with lazy population and explicit invalidation via invalidateRect()
design_decisions.rationale: getBoundingClientRect() forces synchronous layout reflow — calling it on every pointermove (60-1000 Hz) causes significant main-thread jank. Caching the rect and only re-reading on resize eliminates this hot-path thrashing. The invalidateRect() method is exposed on SelectionContext so Viewport.tsx can call it from its existing ResizeObserver, keeping resize handling centralized. Lazy population (first pointer event) avoids a redundant read during construction when the element may not yet be laid out.

design_decisions.decision: Use pending-event + rAF throttle for pointermove raycasting only; pointerdown remains synchronous
design_decisions.rationale: Raycasting is O(n) in mesh count with per-mesh matrix math. At 1000 Hz mouse sampling, synchronous raycasting on every pointermove saturates the main thread. The rAF pattern caps hover raycasts at ~60/s (one per rendered frame), which is perceptually identical. pointerdown (click selection) remains synchronous because: (a) click events are rare (not a hot path), (b) selection feedback must feel instantaneous — a one-frame delay on click is perceptible, and (c) the pending-event pattern would discard intermediate clicks if multiple arrive before the next frame.

design_decisions.decision: Make selectedEntity effect depend on props.meshes to rebuild wireframe on geometry changes
design_decisions.rationale: WireframeGeometry snapshots edge data at construction time — it doesn't track source geometry mutations. When meshManager.sync() updates mesh BufferGeometry after engine re-evaluation, the wireframe becomes stale. By reading props.meshes inside the selectedEntity effect, SolidJS automatically re-runs it whenever meshes change, causing setSelected() to destroy the old wireframe and create a new one from the updated geometry. This is the minimal fix: one added line (`void props.meshes;`) that leverages SolidJS's fine-grained reactivity to solve the staleness without any new mechanism.

reuse.how: Follow same pattern for createSelection: take dependencies as arguments, return context object with methods + dispose.

reuse.what: Three.js vi.mock pattern with inline mock classes
reuse.how: Mock Raycaster, WireframeGeometry, LineSegments, Box3, Vector3, LineBasicMaterial, Color using same vi.mock('three', () => { ... }) pattern.
Selection module uses this map to get raycastable meshes and look up meshes by entity_path for highlight/wireframe. Passed as getMeshes callback.
Viewport wires selection onHover/onSelect callbacks to these store methods, bridging Three.js events to SolidJS reactive state.
Use THEME_TOKENS.accent for wireframe color and hover highlight emissive. Use --reify-* CSS variables for tooltip/spinner styling.
Raycaster intersection returns the mesh whose .name is the entity_path, providing the key for store updates and getMeshes() lookups.
Viewport checks evalStatus.phase !== 'idle' to show/hide the spinner overlay.

reuse.what: Viewport.test.tsx mock patterns (ResizeObserver stub, RAF capture, module-level vi.mock for scene/controls/meshManager)
reuse.how: Extend existing mock setup to include selection module mock. Add tests for new overlay elements.
The mock LineBasicMaterial already has a dispose = vi.fn() — the new material disposal tests can assert on wireframe.material.dispose without any mock changes.

reuse.what: Existing ResizeObserver in Viewport.tsx for resize handling
reuse.how: The ResizeObserver callback already fires on viewport resize. Add selection.invalidateRect() call alongside the existing resize(w, h) call to invalidate the cached DOMRect when the viewport container changes size.
Same rAF mocking pattern will be used in selection.test.ts for the rAF-throttle tests. Capture callbacks via vi.fn() wrapping requestAnimationFrame to verify scheduling and invoke callbacks in tests.
